summary: CC-0050 refactors the CI workflow by extracting inline shell blocks from e2e-infra, e2e-operator, and tempest jobs into 4 reusable scripts (hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh, hack/ci-build-service-image.sh, hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh, hack/ci-run-tempest.sh) and 1 composite action (.github/actions/setup-e2e-infra/action.yaml). The E2E section of ci.yaml was reduced from 379 lines to 184 lines (51% reduction). All job names, needs: dependencies, if: conditions, matrix: strategies, artifact uploads, and timeout-minutes values are preserved. Two incidental cleanup changes (useless cat removal in setup-policies.sh and basename pattern improvement in verify_spdx_headers.sh) are included. Documentation in docs/reference/ci-workflow.md is comprehensive and accurate.

tests_checklist.item: Shellcheck passes on all new scripts (shellcheck unavailable in review env, but scripts follow correct patterns)
No shell test scripts exist for the new CI scripts (acceptable: these are CI orchestration scripts, not logic-testable units)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (SPDX headers, feature references, script structure)
Scripts are parameterized via env vars with sensible defaults
No inline run: block exceeds ~5 lines (acceptance criteria)
All job names, needs:, if:, matrix:, artifacts preserved from original

security_checklist.item: No new security vulnerabilities introduced (pre-existing patterns faithfully reproduced)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
Scripts are usable locally (WORKSPACE_ROOT fallback in ci-run-tempest.sh)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Required env vars validated with :? syntax

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs validated at script boundaries
|| true guards on diagnostic kubectl commands

issues_found.description: ci.yaml is 689 lines, exceeding the acceptance criteria target of <500 lines. The E2E section was reduced from 379 to 184 lines (51% reduction), but the non-E2E section accounts for 505 lines which was not in scope for this refactoring. The acceptance criteria target of <500 lines is unreachable without also refactoring jobs outside the E2E section (changes, lint, test, helm-validate, build-and-push, etc.).
issues_found.fix: This acceptance criterion was aspirational and cannot be met without scope creep into unrelated jobs. Document the actual achievement (884 -> 689 lines, E2E section 379 -> 184 lines) and update the issue to reflect realistic expectations. Alternatively, if the <500 line target is firm, a follow-up issue should be created to extract the 44-line 'Resolve effective changes' step (line 104), the 11-line helm template block (line 304), and the 9-line helm-push block (line 655) into scripts.

issues_found.description: Several inline run: blocks in ci.yaml exceed the ~5 line acceptance criteria. The 'Resolve effective changes' step is 44 lines, 'Helm template' is 11 lines, 'Build Tempest image' is 10 lines, 'Package and push Helm chart' is 9 lines, 'Load images into kind' is 6 lines, and 'Create and push manifest list' is 6 lines. While the first two are in non-E2E jobs (outside refactoring scope), the 'Build Tempest image' (10 lines, line 457) is in the tempest job which was in scope.
issues_found.fix: The 10-line 'Build Tempest image' block at line 457 could be extracted into a script (e.g. hack/ci-build-tempest-image.sh). The non-E2E inline blocks (changes, helm-validate, helm-push) are outside scope. If strict enforcement of the ~5-line rule is required, create a follow-up issue for the remaining blocks.
issues_found.evidence: Lines 457-467: 10-line inline block for building Tempest image within the tempest job (which was in refactoring scope)

issues_found.description: The docs mention 'Scenario 5: namespace-scoped RBAC' for helm-validate (5 scenarios), but the helm-validate docs table only lists 4 scenarios. The actual ci.yaml has 5 scenarios (lines 306-315). The docs were written before the 5th scenario was added in CC-0043 and were not updated.
issues_found.fix: Add Scenario 5 (namespace-scoped RBAC) to the helm-validate docs table. This is a pre-existing docs inconsistency, not introduced by CC-0050.
issues_found.evidence: ci.yaml line 314-315: echo '--- Scenario 5: namespace-scoped RBAC ---' / helm template test "$CHART" --set rbac.namespaceScoped=true --set webhook.enabled=false; docs table has only 4 scenarios

suggested_improvements: Consider extracting the 10-line 'Build Tempest image' block (ci.yaml:457-467) into hack/ci-build-tempest-image.sh for consistency with the other extracted scripts. This block resolves test refs and runs docker build with multiple build args — the same complexity level as ci-build-service-image.sh.

The 44-line 'Resolve effective changes' step (ci.yaml:104-147) is the single largest inline block. While it uses GitHub Actions expression syntax (${{ }}) that complicates extraction, the shell logic portion could potentially be moved to a script with the expression values passed as environment variables. This would be a separate follow-up.

Consider adding input validation for OPERATOR (alphanumeric check) in ci-build-service-image.sh and ci-deploy-operator.sh. While the values are CI-controlled today, validation would harden the scripts for local use as documented.
next_steps: Decide whether the <500 line and ~5 line acceptance criteria should be updated to reflect realistic targets (689 lines total, E2E section well under 200 lines) or if additional extraction work is needed in a follow-up issue.
Optionally extract the 'Build Tempest image' 10-line inline block into a script for full compliance with the ~5-line inline rule within the E2E scope.

detected_patterns.name: CI script with env-var parameterization, SPDX header, and feature ID reference
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new CI helper script under hack/ that encapsulates a sequence of CI steps into a reusable, locally-runnable script
detected_patterns.description: CI helper scripts follow a consistent structure: (1) #!/usr/bin/env bash shebang, (2) SPDX Apache-2.0 header, (3) script name and feature ID in a comment block, (4) required env vars documented with names and descriptions, (5) optional env vars documented with defaults, (6) REQ-nnn references, (7) set -euo pipefail, (8) SCRIPT_DIR/REPO_ROOT computation via BASH_SOURCE for path independence, (9) required vars validated with ${VAR:?message} syntax, (10) optional vars with ${VAR:-default} defaults, (11) numbered section comments (# 1. Description, # 2. Description, etc.) with horizontal rules. All scripts are executable (chmod +x) and designed to work both in CI and locally.

examples.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

# hack/ci-build-service-image.sh — Build an OpenStack service container image.
# Feature: CC-0050
#
# Required env vars:
#   OPERATOR      — OpenStack service name (e.g. keystone)
#   IMAGE_PREFIX  — Container image prefix (e.g. ghcr.io/c5c3)
#
# Optional env vars:
#   RELEASE       — Release directory name (default: 2025.2)
#
# REQ-002: Reusable service image build script.
# REQ-007: set -euo pipefail, SPDX Apache-2.0 header, shellcheck-clean.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"

OPERATOR="${OPERATOR:?OPERATOR is required (e.g. keystone)}"
IMAGE_PREFIX="${IMAGE_PREFIX:?IMAGE_PREFIX is required (e.g. ghcr.io/c5c3)}"
RELEASE="${RELEASE:-2025.2}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

# hack/ci-deploy-operator.sh — Deploy an operator into a kind cluster.
# Feature: CC-0050
#
# Required env vars:
#   OPERATOR    — Operator name (e.g. keystone)
#   IMAGE_REPO  — Full image repository (e.g. ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone-operator)
#
# Optional env vars:
#   IMAGE_TAG   — Image tag (default: dev)
#
# REQ-003: Reusable operator deployment script.
# REQ-007: set -euo pipefail, SPDX Apache-2.0 header, shellcheck-clean.

set -euo pipefail

OPERATOR="${OPERATOR:?OPERATOR is required (e.g. keystone)}"
IMAGE_REPO="${IMAGE_REPO:?IMAGE_REPO is required (e.g. ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone-operator)}"
IMAGE_TAG="${IMAGE_TAG:-dev}"

CHART_PATH="operators/${OPERATOR}/helm/${OPERATOR}-operator"

metadata.title: Refactor CI workflow into reusable scripts and actions

verification.shellcheck: Not available in review environment; scripts follow correct patterns and SPDX header/set -euo pipefail verified manually
verification.functional_equivalence: All job names, needs, if conditions, matrix strategies, artifact uploads, and timeouts verified identical to main branch
verification.security: No new security issues introduced; pre-existing patterns (sed with password, bash -c with variable interpolation) faithfully reproduced from original inline code

Replaced useless cat with input redirection (shellcheck SC2002 fix)
incidental_changes.tests/container-images/verify_spdx_heade: Replaced sed-based basename with parameter expansion
